content: Just as human language learners use deliberate reflexion (the metacognitive vocabulary and writing reflection taught in high school curricula) to consolidate implicit knowledge into explicit, retrievable memory, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to leverage structured reflexion 'language exercises' rather than just open-ended self-critique to improve performance on complex reasoning tasks. This framing recontextualizes LLM self-reflection as a guided learning practice rather than a purely automated validation step.
